title: cerastium gibraltaricum var. boissieri
intro: Cerastium gibraltaricum var. boissieri, also known as the Gibraltar chickweed, is a flowering plant that belongs to the carnation family and is native to the Iberian Peninsula and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, wounds, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Cerastium gibraltaricum var. boissieri include its ability to reduce swelling, promote wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of digestive problems such as diarrhea and ulcers. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or topical ointment, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote overall well-being and treat a range of health conditions.
